qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL @parseRefs leaks unauthorized user attributes through entry reads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same GraphQL `@parseRefs` -> `Elements::parseRefs()` path and missing authorization guard described by the advisory; the finding shows a schema that cannot query users directly still discloses a referenced user attribute.

selected_finding.title: GraphQL @parseRefs leaks unauthorized user attributes through entry reads

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding identifies the advisory's exact GraphQL `@parseRefs` to `Elements::parseRefs()` authorization bypass, and the judge rerun reproduced unauthorized admin-email disclosure through an entry-only schema while direct user queries remained blocked.
evidence: The advisory describes GraphQL `@parseRefs` reaching `craft\services\Elements::parseRefs` without authorization checks; the finding identifies the same `ParseRefs::apply()` -> `Elements::parseRefs()` -> `_getRefTokenReplacement()` path.

In the vulnerable commit, `Gql::_loadGqlDirectives()` registers `ParseRefs::class` by default, and the fixing commit `4d98a07e47580f1712095825d3e3c4d67bc9f8b9` makes `@parseRefs` optional via the `directive:parseRefs` scope, matching the advisory's GraphQL exposure.
The judge-run log shows `DIRECT_USER_QUERY_BLOCKED=yes`, proving the restricted schema could not read users directly through the normal GraphQL path.
The same judge-run log shows `RAW_REFERENCE_TEXT={user:1:email}`, `LEAKED_VALUE=support@craftcms.com`, and `EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS`, proving `@parseRefs` disclosed the admin email anyway.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The original Docker-based reproduction failed before the vulnerable GraphQL path ran because Docker Hub returned unauthenticated pull-rate-limit errors while building the required image.

poc_executions.command: DB_DRIVER=pgsql DB_SERVER=127.0.0.1 DB_PORT=55432 DB_DATABASE=craft_test DB_USER=craft DB_PASSWORD='' DB_SCHEMA=public DB_CHARSET=utf8 DB_COLLATION='' php reproduction/exploit.php /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_d3f3d032/.judge/poc_runs/graphql_parse_refs_idor/target-repo
poc_executions.observed_effect: The host-native fallback installed the pinned vulnerable commit, initialized Craft on local PostgreSQL, confirmed direct users queries were blocked, and then leaked `support@craftcms.com` from `referenceText @parseRefs`.
